name: Coupa API
what_it_does: Enterprise spend management and procurement platform API for automating purchase orders, supplier management, invoice processing, and sourcing events at the enterprise level.
use_cases: Creating and submitting purchase requisitions from workflow automation
Processing and approving supplier invoices through AP automation agents
Querying supplier and contract data for spend analytics
Syncing procurement data with ERP systems (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite)
not_for: Small businesses without enterprise procurement needs
Simple expense tracking (use Expensify or Concur)
best_when: Your enterprise uses Coupa for spend management and you need agents to automate procurement workflows, AP processing, or spend data analytics.
avoid_when: You don't use Coupa or are a small-to-medium business without formal procurement processes.

auth.notes: OAuth2 for third-party integrations. API keys for server-to-server integration. Scopes control access to specific Coupa modules (PO, invoices, suppliers). Keys tied to specific Coupa tenant instance.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; sandbox available for customers
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise contracts; typically $200k-$1M+/year depending on modules
pricing.notes: Enterprise SaaS with annual contracts. Pricing based on business spend under management and modules. API access included with license.

performance.rate_limits: Varies by tenant and plan; typically strict for bulk operations

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: JSON errors with errors array and field-level details. Coupa-specific business rule violations are sometimes cryptic. Documentation quality varies by module.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Purchase orders use external reference numbers for idempotent creation. Invoice processing is not inherently idempotent — must check for duplicates.
known_agent_gotchas: Each Coupa tenant has a unique subdomain URL — must configure per-customer deployment
API version must be specified — older versions return different field names and structures
Approval workflows are business-process driven — API actions may trigger multi-step approvals
PO creation requires specific field mapping to the Coupa data model — field names are non-obvious
Coupa's API documentation is behind a customer portal (Coupa Compass) — requires login to access
Sandbox environment has separate credentials from production — be careful not to mix them

security_score_components.security_notes: Procurement/spend management platform. OAuth2 + API key. Financial procurement data — sensitive. SOC1/SOC2 certified. RBAC controls access to spend data.
